CAL FIRE/RIVERSIDE COUNTY FIREFIGHTERS RESPONDED TO THE REPORT OF AN ADULT FEMALE STRANDED IN THE TEMECULA CREEK ON A SMALL ISLAND WITH RUSHING WATER ON BOTH SIDES. FIREFIGHTERS PERFORMED A WATER RESCUE, SAFELY EXTRACTING THE STRANDED FEMALE AND BRINGING HER TO SAFETY WITH 20 MINUTES AFTER ARRIVAL. PARAMEDICS TRANSPORTED THE PATIENT TO AN AREA HOSPITAL FOR PRECAUTIONARY EVALUATION. .

CAL FIRE/RIVERSIDE COUNTY FIREFIGHTERS RESPONDED TO REPORTS OF A WATER RESCUE AT VAN BUREN BOULEVARD AND 56TH STREET IN JURUPA VALLEY. THE FIRST ARRIVING ENGINE COMPANY REPORTED ONE VEHICLE STUCK IN MUD AND WATER. FIREFIGHTERS EXTRICATED THE TWO OCCUPANTS OF THE VEHICLE. BOTH OCCUPANTS WERE EVALUATED BY PARAMEDICS AND DECLINED FURTHER MEDICAL CARE.

CAL FIRE/RIVERSIDE COUNTY FIREFIGHTERS ARE ON SCENE OF A WATER RESCUE AT HOSTETTLER ROAD AND TEMESCAL CANYON ROAD IN ALBERHILL. THE FIRST ARRIVING ENGINE COMPANY ADVISED FOUR VEHICLES WERE STRANDED IN TWO TO THREE FEET OF STANDING WATER. TWO OF THE VEHICLES WERE OCCUPIED BY ONE VICTIM EACH AND TWO OF THE VEHICLES WERE UNOCCUPIED. FIREFIGHTERS RESCUED BOTH VICTIMS WHO DECLINED FURTHER MEDICAL TREATMENT AFTER BEING EVALUATED BY PARAMEDICS.
